testcase.name: test_jwks_client_get_keys_failure
testcase.failure:
self = <test_jwt.TestJWKSClient object at 0x0000023DB55C9E50> mock_get = <MagicMock name='get' id='2464059382288'>
@patch('auth.jwt.requests.get') def test_jwks_client_get_keys_failure(self, mock_get): """Test JWKS key retrieval failure handling.""" mock_get.side_effect = Exception("Network error")
client = JWKSClient("https://test.supabase.co")
> with pytest.raises(HTTPException) as exc_info: E Failed: DID NOT RAISE <class 'fastapi.exceptions.HTTPException'>
tests\unit\auth\test_jwt.py:68: Failed

testcase.name: test_jwks_client_caching
testcase.failure:
self = <test_jwt.TestJWKSClient object at 0x0000023DB55CA510> mock_get = <MagicMock name='get' id='2464061296144'>
@patch('auth.jwt.requests.get') def test_jwks_client_caching(self, mock_get): """Test that JWKS keys are cached and not fetched multiple times.""" mock_response = Mock() mock_response.raise_for_status.return_value = None mock_response.json.return_value = {"keys": [{"kid": "test", "kty": "RSA"}]} mock_get.return_value = mock_response
client = JWKSClient("https://test.supabase.co")
# Call twice keys1 = client.get_keys() keys2 = client.get_keys()
# Should be same object due to caching assert keys1 is keys2
# Should only call requests.get once > assert mock_get.call_count == 1 E AssertionError: assert 0 == 1 E + where 0 = <MagicMock name='get' id='2464061296144'>.call_count
tests\unit\auth\test_jwt.py:92: AssertionError

testcase.name: test_jwt_audience_mismatch
test_jwt_unsupported_algorithm

failure.message: jose.exceptions.JWKError: Incorrect key type. Expected: 'oct', Received: RSA

testcase.name: test_jwks_cache_invalidation_on_key_not_found

def _calculate_actual_payment(self, investor: Investor) -> float: """ Calculate the actual payment amount for an investor based on plan parameters. """ p = self.params start_round = investor.start_company_round
# Get the scheduled payment amount for this start round > scheduled_payment = p['scheduled_payment'].get(start_round, 0) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ E KeyError: 'scheduled_payment'

testcase.name: test_plan_a_canonical_snapshot
testcase.failure:
self = <test_simulation_service.TestSimulationServiceMultiRoundSnapshot object at 0x0000023DB586E7D0> simulation_service_factory = <function simulation_service_factory.<locals>._create_service at 0x0000023DB572F740> determinism_guard = None
def test_plan_a_canonical_snapshot(self, simulation_service_factory, determinism_guard): """Test Plan A with canonical 36 rounds and validate against snapshot.""" from conftest import CANONICAL_SNAPSHOT_ROUNDS
# Use determinism guard to ensure no randomness svc = simulation_service_factory('A') > result = svc.run_simulation(CANONICAL_SNAPSHOT_ROUNDS) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
tests\unit\simulation\test_simulation_service.py:69: _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ simulation_service.py:347: in run_simulation self.run_single_round() simulation_service.py:260: in run_single_round actual_payment = self._calculate_actual_payment(investor) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _

# Get the scheduled payment amount for this start round > scheduled_payment = p['scheduled_payment'].get(start_round, 0) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ E KeyError: 'scheduled_payment'

testcase.name: test_cumulative_tax_accumulation
testcase.failure:
self = <test_simulation_service.TestSimulationServiceTaxLogic object at 0x0000023DB5860CD0> simulation_service_factory = <function simulation_service_factory.<locals>._create_service at 0x0000023DB5780860>
def test_cumulative_tax_accumulation(self, simulation_service_factory): """Test that cumulative tax calculations don't double-tax.""" svc = simulation_service_factory('A') > result = svc.run_simulation(3) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^

def _calculate_actual_payment(self, investor: Investor) -> float: """ Calculate the actual payment amount for an investor based on plan parameters. """ p = self.params start_round = investor.start_company_round

testcase.name: test_scheduled_payment_negative_values_handling

failure.message: assert False + where False = all(<generator object TestSimulationServiceInputValidation.test_scheduled_payment_negative_values_handling.<locals>.<genexpr> at 0x0000023DB56105F0>)

testcase.failure:
self = <test_simulation_service.TestSimulationServiceInputValidation object at 0x0000023DB5880950> simulation_service_factory = <function simulation_service_factory.<locals>._create_service at 0x0000023DB5780F40>
def test_scheduled_payment_negative_values_handling(self, simulation_service_factory): """Test handling of negative payment values.""" # The service should handle this gracefully by using minimums negative_payments = {1: -100000, 2: 200000} svc = simulation_service_factory('A', {'scheduled_payment': negative_payments})
# Should use minimum payment rules instead of negative values result = svc.run_simulation(2) assert len(result.history) == 2 > assert all(r.total_payment >= 0 for r in result.history) E assert False E + where False = all(<generator object TestSimulationServiceInputValidation.test_scheduled_payment_negative_values_handling.<locals>.<genexpr> at 0x0000023DB56105F0>)
tests\unit\simulation\test_simulation_service.py:381: AssertionError

testcase.name: test_zero_payment_handling
testcase.failure:
self = <test_simulation_service.TestSimulationServiceMonetaryExtremes object at 0x0000023DB5861810> simulation_service_factory = <function simulation_service_factory.<locals>._create_service at 0x0000023DB5780680>
def test_zero_payment_handling(self, simulation_service_factory): """Test handling of zero payment values.""" zero_payments = {1: 0, 2: 0, 3: 0} svc = simulation_service_factory('A', {'scheduled_payment': zero_payments})
result = svc.run_simulation(3)
# Should use minimum payment rules instead of zero assert len(result.history) == 3 # Payments should fall back to minimums from plan parameters for round_result in result.history: > assert round_result.total_payment > 0 # Should use minimums ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ E assert 0 > 0 E + where 0 = <simulation_service.SimulationRoundResult object at 0x0000023DB58FD290>.total_payment
tests\unit\simulation\test_simulation_service.py:427: AssertionError

testcase.name: test_phone_invalid_prefix_raises
testcase.failure:
self = <test_utils.TestPhoneNormalizationUtility object at 0x0000023DB5675E10>
def test_phone_invalid_prefix_raises(self): """Invalid Korean mobile prefixes should raise ValueError (strict policy).""" from services.otp.utils import normalize_phone
with pytest.raises(ValueError): normalize_phone("020-1234-5678") with pytest.raises(ValueError): normalize_phone("012-1234-5678") > with pytest.raises(ValueError): E Failed: DID NOT RAISE <class 'ValueError'>
tests\unit\utils\test_utils.py:98: Failed
